tasks.title: Grid System & Arena Setup
tasks.description: [code] Define the grid-based coordinate system, cell size, arena dimensions, and provide grid ↔ pixel conversion utilities
tasks.details:
Create `scripts/grid.gd` as a static class or autoload:
- Define constants: CELL_SIZE (e.g., 32px), GRID_WIDTH (20), GRID_HEIGHT (15)
- Implement `cell_to_pixel(cell: Vector2i) -> Vector2` returning top-left corner of the cell
- Implement `pixel_to_cell(pos: Vector2) -> Vector2i` for reverse conversion
- Implement `is_valid_cell(cell: Vector2i) -> bool` checking 0 <= x < GRID_WIDTH and 0 <= y < GRID_HEIGHT
- Implement `is_border_cell(cell: Vector2i) -> bool` returning true if cell is on the outer edge (x==0 or x==GRID_WIDTH-1 or y==0 or y==GRID_HEIGHT-1)

Pseudo-code:
```gdscript
class_name Grid
const CELL_SIZE = 32
const GRID_WIDTH = 20
const GRID_HEIGHT = 15

static func cell_to_pixel(cell: Vector2i) -> Vector2:
    return Vector2(cell.x * CELL_SIZE, cell.y * CELL_SIZE)

static func is_border_cell(cell: Vector2i) -> bool:
    return cell.x == 0 or cell.x == GRID_WIDTH - 1 or cell.y == 0 or cell.y == GRID_HEIGHT - 1
```
tasks.testStrategy: Unit test grid conversions, boundary checks, and border detection

tasks.title: Snake Data Model
tasks.description: [code] Implement the snake as an ordered list of grid cells (head-first) with direction tracking
tasks.details:
Create `scripts/snake.gd`:
- `var body: Array[Vector2i]` storing segments with head at index 0
- `var direction: Vector2i` current movement direction (one of Vector2i.UP/DOWN/LEFT/RIGHT)
- `func _init()` initializes snake at center (Grid.GRID_WIDTH/2, Grid.GRID_HEIGHT/2) with 3 segments facing right
- `func get_head() -> Vector2i` returns body[0]
- `func move_forward()` adds new head at (head + direction), removes tail
- `func grow()` adds new head without removing tail
- `func set_direction(new_dir: Vector2i)` with 180-degree reversal prevention: if body.size() > 1 and new_dir == -direction, reject
- `func is_colliding_with_self() -> bool` checks if head position exists in body[1..end]

Pseudo-code:
```gdscript
class_name Snake
var body: Array[Vector2i] = []
var direction := Vector2i.RIGHT

func _init():
    var start := Vector2i(Grid.GRID_WIDTH / 2, Grid.GRID_HEIGHT / 2)
    body = [start, start - Vector2i.RIGHT, start - Vector2i.RIGHT * 2]

func move_forward():
    body.insert(0, body[0] + direction)
    body.pop_back()

func set_direction(new_dir: Vector2i):
    if body.size() > 1 and new_dir == -direction:
        return  # reject 180 reversal
    direction = new_dir
```
tasks.testStrategy: Unit test initialization, movement, growth, self-collision detection, and 180-degree reversal prevention

tasks.title: Game Controller & Tick System
tasks.description: [code] Create the main game controller with fixed-interval tick timer and game state management
tasks.details:
Create `scripts/game_controller.gd` as the root script for Main.tscn:
- Enum `GameState { PLAYING, GAME_OVER }`
- `var state: GameState = GameState.PLAYING`
- `var snake: Snake`
- `var score: int = 0`
- `var high_score: int = 0`
- `var tick_timer: Timer`
- `func _ready()` initializes snake, creates Timer node with wait_time=0.15, one_shot=false, connects timeout to `_on_tick()`
- `func _on_tick()` executes one game step: move snake, check collisions, update food
- `func reset_game()` reinitializes snake, score, clears food, sets state to PLAYING, starts timer
- `func game_over()` stops timer, plays death sound, updates high_score if needed, sets state to GAME_OVER

Pseudo-code:
```gdscript
extends Node2D
enum GameState { PLAYING, GAME_OVER }
var state := GameState.PLAYING
var snake: Snake
var tick_timer: Timer

func _ready():
    snake = Snake.new()
    tick_timer = Timer.new()
    tick_timer.wait_time = 0.15
    tick_timer.one_shot = false
    tick_timer.timeout.connect(_on_tick)
    add_child(tick_timer)
    tick_timer.start()

func _on_tick():
    if state != GameState.PLAYING: return
    snake.move_forward()
    # collision & food checks follow
```
tasks.testStrategy: Unit test game state transitions, tick execution, score tracking, and reset functionality

tasks.title: Input Handling
tasks.description: [code] Map Arrow keys and WASD to absolute 4-direction control with 180-degree reversal prevention
tasks.testStrategy: Unit test direction changes, reversal rejection with snake length > 1, and game-over restart input

tasks.title: Food Spawning System
tasks.description: [code] Spawn food at random empty (non-wall, non-snake) grid cells and handle respawning on consumption
tasks.details:
In `scripts/game_controller.gd`:
- `var food_position: Vector2i` stores current food cell
- `func spawn_food()` finds random empty cell:
  - Collect all valid cells (0 < x < GRID_WIDTH-1, 0 < y < GRID_HEIGHT-1) excluding snake body
  - Randomly select one: `food_position = empty_cells.pick_random()`
- `func check_food_collision()` in `_on_tick()`:
  - If snake.get_head() == food_position: snake.grow(), score += 1, play eat sound, spawn_food()

Pseudo-code:
```gdscript
var food_position: Vector2i

func spawn_food():
    var empty_cells: Array[Vector2i] = []
    for x in range(1, Grid.GRID_WIDTH - 1):
        for y in range(1, Grid.GRID_HEIGHT - 1):
            var cell := Vector2i(x, y)
            if not snake.body.has(cell):
                empty_cells.append(cell)
    food_position = empty_cells.pick_random()

func _on_tick():
    snake.move_forward()
    if snake.get_head() == food_position:
        snake.grow()
        score += 1
        spawn_food()
```
tasks.testStrategy: Unit test food spawning excludes walls and snake body, collision detection triggers growth and scoring

tasks.title: Collision Detection
tasks.description: [code] Detect wall and self-collision; trigger game over on collision
tasks.details:
In `scripts/game_controller.gd`, in `_on_tick()` after moving:
- Check wall collision: `if Grid.is_border_cell(snake.get_head()): game_over()`
- Check self-collision: `if snake.is_colliding_with_self(): game_over()`

Order in `_on_tick()`:
1. snake.move_forward()
2. Check wall collision
3. Check self-collision
4. Check food collision (grow if eating)

Pseudo-code:
```gdscript
func _on_tick():
    if state != GameState.PLAYING: return
    snake.move_forward()
    var head := snake.get_head()
    if Grid.is_border_cell(head) or snake.is_colliding_with_self():
        game_over()
        return
    if head == food_position:
        snake.grow()
        score += 1
        spawn_food()
```
tasks.testStrategy: Unit test wall collision triggers game over, self-collision triggers game over, eating does not trigger collision

tasks.title: Visual Rendering - Floor & Walls
tasks.description: [art] Render grid floor tiles and deadly wall border using TileMap or sprite positioning
tasks.details:
In Main.tscn, add a TileMap node or use individual Sprite2D nodes:

Option A (TileMap - recommended):
- Create TileMap node as child of Main
- Create TileSet resource with two tiles:
  - Tile 0: `source/sprites/floor_tile.png` for interior
  - Tile 1: `source/sprites/wall_tile.png` for border
- In `_ready()` of game_controller.gd, populate TileMap:
  - For each cell in grid, set border cells to tile 1, interior to tile 0

Option B (Sprite2D array):
- For each grid cell, instantiate Sprite2D at Grid.cell_to_pixel(cell)
- Use `floor_tile.png` for interior, `wall_tile.png` for border
- Parent all to a "Background" Node2D

Pseudo-code (TileMap):
```gdscript
var tile_map: TileMap

func _ready():
    tile_map = $TileMap  # reference TileMap child
    for x in Grid.GRID_WIDTH:
        for y in Grid.GRID_HEIGHT:
            var cell := Vector2i(x, y)
            var tile_id := 1 if Grid.is_border_cell(cell) else 0
            tile_map.set_cell(0, cell, 0, Vector2i(tile_id, 0))
```

Note: Assets must exist at `source/sprites/floor_tile.png` and `source/sprites/wall_tile.png`. Create placeholder assets if needed.

tasks.title: Visual Rendering - Snake & Food
tasks.description: [art] Render snake head, body segments, and food sprite with proper positioning and rotation
tasks.details:
In `scripts/game_controller.gd`, add visual node management:
- Preload sprites: `const HEAD_SPRITE = preload("res://source/sprites/player_head.png")`, `const BODY_SPRITE = preload("res://source/sprites/player_body.png")`, `const FOOD_SPRITE = preload("res://source/sprites/food.png")`
- Create container nodes in `_ready()`: `var snake_visuals: Node2D`, `var food_sprite: Sprite2D`
- `func update_visuals()`:
  - Clear and recreate snake sprites:
    - For each segment in snake.body, create Sprite2D at Grid.cell_to_pixel(segment) + offset to center
    - Head (index 0) uses HEAD_SPRITE with rotation based on direction: UP=-90°, DOWN=90°, LEFT=180°, RIGHT=0°
    - Body segments use BODY_SPRITE (no rotation needed)
  - Update food sprite position to Grid.cell_to_pixel(food_position) + center offset
- Call `update_visuals()` in `_on_tick()` after all game logic

Pseudo-code:
```gdscript
func update_visuals():
    # Clear old snake sprites
    for child in snake_visuals.get_children(): child.queue_free()
    
    # Render snake
    for i in snake.body.size():
        var sprite := Sprite2D.new()
        sprite.texture = HEAD_SPRITE if i == 0 else BODY_SPRITE
        sprite.position = Grid.cell_to_pixel(snake.body[i]) + Vector2(Grid.CELL_SIZE/2, Grid.CELL_SIZE/2)
        if i == 0:  # rotate head
            sprite.rotation_degrees = {Vector2i.UP: -90, Vector2i.DOWN: 90, Vector2i.LEFT: 180, Vector2i.RIGHT: 0}[snake.direction]
        snake_visuals.add_child(sprite)
    
    # Update food
    food_sprite.position = Grid.cell_to_pixel(food_position) + Vector2(Grid.CELL_SIZE/2, Grid.CELL_SIZE/2)
```

Note: Create placeholder sprites if assets don't exist yet.

tasks.title: UI - Score & Game Over Display
tasks.description: [code] Display live score, high score, and game-over message with restart prompt
tasks.details:
In Main.tscn, add Label nodes:
- `ScoreLabel` (top-left): shows "Score: {score}" during play
- `GameOverLabel` (center): hidden during play, shows "GAME OVER\nScore: {score}\nHigh Score: {high_score}\nPress any key to restart" when dead

In `scripts/game_controller.gd`:
- Reference labels: `@onready var score_label: Label = $ScoreLabel`, `@onready var game_over_label: Label = $GameOverLabel`
- `func update_ui()`:
  - `score_label.text = "Score: %d" % score`
  - `score_label.visible = (state == GameState.PLAYING)`
  - `game_over_label.visible = (state == GameState.GAME_OVER)`
  - If game over: `game_over_label.text = "GAME OVER\nScore: %d\nHigh Score: %d\nPress any key to restart" % [score, high_score]`
- Call `update_ui()` in `_on_tick()`, `game_over()`, and `reset_game()`

Pseudo-code:
```gdscript
func update_ui():
    score_label.text = "Score: %d" % score
    score_label.visible = (state == GameState.PLAYING)
    game_over_label.visible = (state == GameState.GAME_OVER)
    if state == GameState.GAME_OVER:
        game_over_label.text = "GAME OVER\nScore: %d\nHigh Score: %d\nPress any key" % [score, high_score]

func game_over():
    state = GameState.GAME_OVER
    tick_timer.stop()
    high_score = max(high_score, score)
    update_ui()
```

tasks.title: Audio - Eat & Death Sounds
tasks.description: [audio] Play sound effects for eating food and dying using AudioStreamPlayer
tasks.details:
In Main.tscn, add two AudioStreamPlayer nodes:
- `EatSound`: load `source/audio/eat_food.wav`
- `DeathSound`: load `source/audio/death.wav`

In `scripts/game_controller.gd`:
- Reference players: `@onready var eat_sound: AudioStreamPlayer = $EatSound`, `@onready var death_sound: AudioStreamPlayer = $DeathSound`
- In `_on_tick()` when food is eaten: `eat_sound.play()`
- In `game_over()`: `death_sound.play()`

Pseudo-code:
```gdscript
func _on_tick():
    # ... movement & collision ...
    if snake.get_head() == food_position:
        snake.grow()
        score += 1
        eat_sound.play()
        spawn_food()

func game_over():
    death_sound.play()
    state = GameState.GAME_OVER
    # ...
```

Note: Create placeholder audio files if `source/audio/` doesn't exist.

tasks.title: Golden Path Integration Test
tasks.description: [code] Create an integration test that simulates a complete game session from start to game over
tasks.details:
Create `test/golden_path_test.gd` extending GdUnitTestSuite:
- Test simulates: game start → input commands → eating food → growing → collision → game over → restart
- Use GdUnitSceneRunner to run Main.tscn:
  ```gdscript
  var runner := scene_runner("res://Main.tscn")
  var controller := runner.get_property("game_controller")
  ```
- Simulate inputs by directly calling controller methods or injecting InputEvents
- Assert: initial snake length == 3, score == 0
- Simulate movement toward food, assert snake grows and score increments
- Simulate collision with wall or self, assert state == GAME_OVER
- Simulate restart input, assert score resets and state == PLAYING

Pseudo-code:
```gdscript
extends GdUnitTestSuite

func test_golden_path_full_game_cycle() -> void:
    var runner := scene_runner("res://Main.tscn")
    var controller = runner.get_property(".")
    
    # Verify initial state
    assert_int(controller.snake.body.size()).is_equal(3)
    assert_int(controller.score).is_equal(0)
    
    # Simulate eating food
    controller.snake.direction = Vector2i.RIGHT
    var initial_length := controller.snake.body.size()
    # ... manually trigger tick, food collision ...
    assert_int(controller.snake.body.size()).is_greater(initial_length)
    assert_int(controller.score).is_greater(0)
    
    # Simulate wall collision
    controller.snake.body[0] = Vector2i(0, 5)  # move to wall
    controller._on_tick()
    assert_that(controller.state).is_equal(controller.GameState.GAME_OVER)
    
    # Simulate restart
    controller.reset_game()
    assert_int(controller.score).is_equal(0)
    assert_that(controller.state).is_equal(controller.GameState.PLAYING)
```
tasks.testStrategy: Integration test covering full game loop: movement, eating, collision, game over, restart
